title: Short Quiz
bundle_title: RL.7.2 Theme and Central Idea Bundle
tagline: Analyze Themes | Identify Central Ideas | Support with Evidence
objectives:
• Determine the theme or central idea of a literary text
• Analyze the development of theme throughout the text
• Cite textual evidence to support analysis of theme
directions: Directions: Choose the best answer for questions 1-5. Write your response for questions 6-7.

questions.question: What is the difference between theme and topic?

options.A: Theme is what the story is about; topic is the lesson learned
options.B: Theme is the lesson or message; topic is what the story is about
options.C: Theme and topic mean the same thing
options.D: Theme is only found in fiction; topic is only found in nonfiction

questions.answer: B - Theme is the lesson or message; topic is what the story is about

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a common theme in literature?

options.C: The story takes place in a school
options.D: Growing up requires making difficult choices

questions.answer: C - The story takes place in a school

questions.question: How does an author typically develop theme throughout a story?

options.A: By stating the theme directly in the first paragraph
options.B: Through character actions, dialogue, and plot events
options.C: By repeating the same sentence multiple times

questions.answer: B - Through character actions, dialogue, and plot events

questions.question: When analyzing theme, which question should you ask yourself?

options.C: What lesson does the main character learn?

questions.answer: C - What lesson does the main character learn?

questions.question: Which of these statements best represents a theme rather than a topic?

options.B: Standing up for others takes courage
options.D: The setting is a middle school

questions.answer: B - Standing up for others takes courage

questions.question: Explain how a character's dialogue can reveal the theme of a story. Provide an example.
questions.answer: A character's dialogue can reveal theme through the words they speak and the lessons they express. For example, if a character says 'I learned that being honest is more important than being popular,' this dialogue directly reveals a theme about the importance of honesty and integrity.

questions.question: Describe how you would use textual evidence to support your analysis of a story's theme.
questions.answer: To support theme analysis with textual evidence, I would identify specific quotes, character actions, or plot events that demonstrate the theme. I would explain how each piece of evidence connects to the theme and shows its development throughout the story.
